model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Lehigh gets a slight edge because the underlying predictive metrics are a bit better overall, with both Torvik and KenPom rating the Mountain Hawks narrowly ahead of Prairie View A&M. ESPN’s matchup predictor also leans Lehigh, and Prairie View’s offense grades a little worse, which matters in a game between two similarly seeded, lower-efficiency teams. Prairie View has a credible path if its slightly better defense controls the game, but Lehigh looks like the marginally safer pick in a near toss-up.

reasoningStep.title: Lehigh over Prairie View A&M in a close First Four game
reasoningStep.summary: This projects as a very tight 16-seed play-in, but Lehigh holds a small edge in the combined predictive profile and external win probability. Prairie View A&M is live because its defense rates a touch better, yet its weaker offense and slightly lower overall ratings make it the riskier side.
evidence: KenPom: Lehigh No. 284 (AdjEM -10.38) vs Prairie View A&M No. 291 (AdjEM -11.1).
Torvik: Lehigh No. 282 vs Prairie View A&M No. 292.

Offense/defense split: Lehigh has the slightly better offense (KP AdjO 102.8 vs 101.1; Torvik 102.53 vs 101.04), while Prairie View has the slightly better defense (KP AdjD 112.2 vs 113.1; Torvik 111.90 vs 112.75).
ESPN matchup predictor listed Lehigh at 53.6% vs Prairie View A&M at 46.4%.
Web/news checks did not surface a major availability issue strong enough to materially change the baseline projection.

You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
